name: Gulab
description: A draped wrap dress in raw silk organza, inspired by the way motia petals fold inward at dusk. The silhouette references the dupatta — worn loose, unhurried, and close to the skin.

name: Mehak
description: A layered tunic in handwoven khadi with pressed motia flowers embedded into the weave. The garment carries the flower literally — its texture visible in the cloth.
materials: Handwoven khadi, pressed flowers, cotton lining

name: Chameli
description: A wide-leg trouser in washed cotton with a drawstring waist. The fabric has been tea-stained to achieve the exact off-white of a motia bud before it opens.

name: Rani
description: A structured jacket with hand-embroidered motia motifs along the collar and cuffs. The embroidery follows the pattern of a traditional gajra — flowers threaded in a continuous line.

name: Nanima
description: A floor-length slip dress in cream silk charmeuse, named for the grandmother. The simplest piece in the collection — it carries nothing except the warmth of its colour and the weight of its name.

name: Gajra
description: A hand-knitted shawl with a border of crocheted motia flowers. Intended to be worn as nano wears her chadar — draped over the shoulders during evening prayer.

name: Khushbu
description: An open-front vest in naturally dyed linen, with interior pockets designed to hold dried motia sachets. The scent becomes part of the garment's life.
materials: Linen, natural indigo and turmeric dye

name: Bela
description: A cropped blouse with pintuck detailing that mirrors the veins of a jasmine leaf. Designed to be worn with Chameli — together they form a quiet, complete look.
